text_en: Noting that Italy has been entrusted by the United Nations with the administration of the Trust Territory of Somalia and that it is currently exercising its responsibilities to the United Nations for the administration of the Trust Territory of Somalia in accordance with the provisions of Chapters XII and XIII of the Charter,

text_en: Considering, therefore, that Italy should become a member of the Council, and that for this purpose it should be accepted as a member of the Council, and considering, moreover, that Italy fulfils the conditions which Article 4(1) of the Charter requires for this admission,
text_fr: Estimant en consequence  necessaire  que l'Italie devienne membre du Conseil  <le  tutelle et qu'a cette fin elle  soit  admise  clans  !'Organisation des  Nations Cnies, et  considerant  en  outre  que  l'Italie remplit  Jes  condi› tions  que  le  paragraphe  premier  de  !'Article 4  de  la Charte exige  pour  cette  admission,

Section  A

text_en: 1. Chapter XI of the Charter of the United Nations constitutes a declaration relating to the Non-Self-Governing Territories. Article 73 e, which forms part of this chapter, provides that Members of the United Nations who have or assume responsibility for the administration of Territories whose populations do not yet fully administer themselves, undertake to:
text_fr: 1. Le  Chapitre  XI  de  la  Charte des  Nations  Unies constitue  une  declaration  relative  aux.  Territoires  non autonomes.  Aux  termes de !'Article 73  e,  qui  fait  partie de ce  chapitre, les  Membres des  Nations  Unies  qui  ont ou  qui  assument  la  responsabilite  d'administrer  des Territoires  dont  Jes  populations  ne  s 'administrent pas encore  completement  elles-memes,  s'engagent  a :

text_en: "to communicate regularly to the Secretary General. for information, subject to the requirements of security and constitutional considerations, statistical and other information of a technical nature relating to economic, social and educational conditions in the territories for which they are respectively responsible, other than those to which Chapters XII and XIII apply".
text_fr: « communiquer regulierement au Secretaire general. a titre d'information, sous reserve des  exigences  de  la securite  et  de  considerations  d 'ordre constitutionnel, des  renseignements  statistiques  et  autres  de  nature technique relatifs aux conditions economiques, sociales et  de  !'instruction dans  Jes  territoires  dont  ils  sont respectivement responsables, autres que ceux auxquels s'appliquent les  Chapitres  XII  et  XIII».

text_en: 2. By its Resolution 142 (II) of 3 November 1947, the General Assembly approved the establishment of a "Schema" intended to serve as a guide for Member States in the preparation of the information to be transmitted under Article 73 (III) of 3 November 1948, it has developed the previous resolution and has recommended in particular, at the same time as it invited Member States to transmit the more recent information available to them, that they make known the changes which have been made in statistical data and any other significant changes, including the progress made in the implementation of the development programmes; these amendments are referred to in the previous year, and concern the problems which are aimed at in Article 73 (e) of the Charter. It has stated that it is not necessary to retrace the information previously provided if it is taken care to refer to the complete data sources.

Par  sa  resolution  142  (II)  du  3  novembre  1947, I' Assemblee  generale  a  approuve  l'etablissement d 'un « Schema » destine a servir de guide aux Etats Membres pour  la  preparation  des  renseignements  a transmettre en vertu de  !'Article 73  e. text_en: 1. The points mentioned in the Schema sometimes relate to permanent factors such as the area or geographical location of a given Territory. In this case there would be no interest in reproducing information already provided. This also applies to certain characteristics such as natural resources or the main features of the economy of a land; ii it would be useful to mention here changes observed, for example, after geological prospecting or the introduction of new cultures.
